concerns.text: T034 only mandates calculating 'empirical Type I error rates'. FR-004 and SC-002 explicitly require calculating 'confidence interval coverage rates' (proportion of intervals containing the true value). The task list omits the implementation of this core success criterion, effectively weakening the requirement to only measure Type I error.

T030 implements 'Sample Mean Deviation' (SC-003) but fails to include the calculation of 'CI coverage' (SC-002). The spec requires measuring CI coverage against known population parameters (FR-006/SC-002). By only addressing bias/deviation, the task list silently drops the acceptance criterion for CI coverage measurement.

T019 hardcodes a specific list of 10 datasets (Iris, Wine, etc.). FR-001 requires '5-10 diverse public datasets... including at least 2 numerical and 2 categorical'. Hardcoding a specific list risks violating the 'diverse' and 'categorical' constraints if the listed datasets do not meet the mix (e.g., if a listed dataset is purely numerical). The task should implement a selection logic or verify the list meets the diversity constraint, rather than assuming the hardcoded list is sufficient.

T031 implements listwise deletion (dropna) as per the spec's Assumptions. However, the Assumptions section explicitly states the system must 'measure both power loss and Type I error stability to distinguish these effects'. T031 performs the deletion but no corresponding task exists to measure or report the resulting reduction in statistical power (sample size loss). This drops the acceptance criterion for measuring power loss.
